title: The Barber's Mirror
text_zh: 理发店的镜子里可以看到很多东西。有人坐在椅子上看手机，不说话。有人跟理发的人聊天，聊得很高兴。我喜欢从镜子里看外面的街——人在走，车在过，但是在镜子里看起来好像是另一个世界。
text_pinyin: Lǐfà diàn de jìngzi lǐ kěyǐ kàn dào hěn duō dōngxi. Yǒu rén zuò zài yǐzi shàng kàn shǒujī, bù shuō huà. Yǒu rén gēn lǐfà de rén liáotiān, liáo de hěn gāoxìng. Wǒ xǐhuan cóng jìngzi lǐ kàn wàimiàn de jiē——rén zài zǒu, chē zài guò, dànshì zài jìngzi lǐ kàn qǐlái hǎoxiàng shì lìng yí gè shìjiè.
text_en: You can see a lot in the barbershop mirror. Some sit in the chair looking at phones, saying nothing. Some chat happily with the barber. I like watching the street outside through the mirror — people walking, cars passing, but in the mirror it looks like another world.

questions.q_zh: 有的人在理发店做什么？
questions.q_en: What do some people do at the barbershop?
What does 'I' like to see in the mirror?

questions.q_zh: 在镜子里看外面的感觉是什么？
questions.q_en: What does the street look like in the mirror?

title: The Cigarette Break
text_zh: 公司楼下有一个地方，大家都去那里抽烟。虽然我不抽烟，但是有时候也去那里站一会儿。因为在那里，不同部门的人会说话，而且说的都是真话。五分钟的休息时间，我听到的比开会的时候多。
text_pinyin: Gōngsī lóu xià yǒu yí gè dìfang, dàjiā dōu qù nàli chōu yān. Suīrán wǒ bù chōu yān, dànshì yǒu shíhou yě qù nàli zhàn yíhuìr. Yīnwèi zài nàli, bùtóng bùmén de rén huì shuō huà, érqiě shuō de dōu shì zhēn huà. Wǔ fēnzhōng de xiūxi shíjiān, wǒ tīng dào de bǐ kāi huì de shíhou duō.
text_en: Downstairs from the office there's a spot where everyone goes to smoke. Although I don't smoke, sometimes I stand there for a while. Because there, people from different departments talk, and they tell the truth. In five minutes of break time, I hear more than in meetings.

questions.q_en: Does 'I' smoke?

questions.q_zh: 在抽烟的地方，人们说话有什么特别的？
questions.q_en: What's special about how people talk there?

options.pinyin: shuō de dōu shì zhēn huà

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得在那里听到的跟开会比怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' compare it to meetings?

options.pinyin: tīng dào de bǐ kāi huì shǎo
tīng dào de bǐ kāi huì duō

title: The Old Couple on the Bench
text_zh: 每天下午，公园的长椅上都坐着两个老人。他们不怎么说话，有时候一个人看报纸，另一个人看远方。但是他们总是坐得很近。有一次我看到老太太冷了，老先生什么都没说，把自己的外套给了她。好像几十年了，他们已经不需要说话了。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān xiàwǔ, gōngyuán de cháng yǐ shàng dōu zuòzhe liǎng gè lǎorén. Tāmen bù zěnme shuō huà, yǒu shíhou yí gè rén kàn bàozhǐ, lìng yí gè rén kàn yuǎnfāng. Dànshì tāmen zǒngshì zuò de hěn jìn. Yǒu yí cì wǒ kàn dào lǎo tàitai lěng le, lǎo xiānsheng shénme dōu méi shuō, bǎ zìjǐ de wàitào gěi le tā. Hǎoxiàng jǐ shí nián le, tāmen yǐjīng bù xūyào shuō huà le.
text_en: Every afternoon, two old people sit on the park bench. They don't talk much — one reads the newspaper, the other looks into the distance. But they always sit close together. Once I saw the old lady was cold. The old man said nothing, just gave her his coat. As if after decades, they no longer need words.

questions.q_zh: 两个老人在公园做什么？
questions.q_en: What do the two old people do in the park?

options.text: 坐在长椅上，不怎么说话
options.pinyin: zuò zài cháng yǐ shàng, bù zěnme shuō huà
options.text_en: sit on the bench, don't talk much

questions.q_zh: 老太太冷的时候，老先生怎么做了？
questions.q_en: What did the old man do when the lady was cold?

options.pinyin: wèn tā yào bu yào huí jiā
options.text_en: asked if she wanted to go home

options.text: 什么都没说，把外套给了她
options.pinyin: shénme dōu méi shuō, bǎ wàitào gěi le tā
options.text_en: said nothing, gave her his coat

options.pinyin: qù mǎi le yì bēi rè chá

questions.q_en: Why does 'I' think they don't talk?

options.text: 因为几十年了，不需要说话了
options.pinyin: yīnwèi jǐ shí nián le, bù xūyào shuō huà le
options.text_en: after decades, no longer need words

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tāmen bú huì shuō huà
yīnwèi gōngyuán lǐ bù néng shuō huà
options.text_en: because you can't talk in the park

title: The Delivery Driver
text_zh: 每天中午，送外卖的人很忙。他们骑着车，在车和人中间穿过去。有一次我在楼下等外卖，那个人跑上来的时候满头都是汗。他笑着把饭给我，说：「还好没有晚。」我说了谢谢，但是觉得只说谢谢好像不够。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zhōngwǔ, sòng wàimài de rén hěn máng. Tāmen qízhe chē, zài chē hé rén zhōngjiān chuān guòqù. Yǒu yí cì wǒ zài lóu xià děng wàimài, nàge rén pǎo shànglái de shíhou mǎn tóu dōu shì hàn. Tā xiàozhe bǎ fàn gěi wǒ, shuō:「Hái hǎo méiyǒu wǎn.」Wǒ shuō le xièxie, dànshì juéde zhǐ shuō xièxie hǎoxiàng bú gòu.
text_en: Every noon, delivery drivers are busy. They ride through traffic and crowds. Once I waited downstairs for a delivery. When the driver ran up, his head was covered in sweat. He smiled, handed me the food, and said: 'Good thing I wasn't late.' I said thank you, but felt like just saying thank you wasn't enough.

questions.q_zh: 送外卖的人中午怎么样？
questions.q_en: How are delivery drivers at noon?

questions.q_zh: 那个人跑上来的时候看起来怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did the driver look when he ran up?

questions.q_zh: 「我」说了谢谢以后觉得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel after saying thank you?

options.pinyin: juéde zhǐ shuō xièxie bú gòu

title: The Stray Cat's Schedule
text_zh: 小区里有一只流浪猫。它好像有自己的时间表：早上在一楼老太太门口吃东西，中午在三楼的阳台上睡觉，下午在花园里走来走去。虽然没有人是它的主人，但是好像每个人都在照顾它。它比我们都自由。
text_pinyin: Xiǎoqū lǐ yǒu yì zhī liúlàng māo. Tā hǎoxiàng yǒu zìjǐ de shíjiānbiǎo: zǎoshang zài yī lóu lǎo tàitai ménkǒu chī dōngxi, zhōngwǔ zài sān lóu de yángtái shàng shuì jiào, xiàwǔ zài huāyuán lǐ zǒu lái zǒu qù. Suīrán méiyǒu rén shì tā de zhǔrén, dànshì hǎoxiàng měi gè rén dōu zài zhàogù tā. Tā bǐ wǒmen dōu zìyóu.
text_en: There's a stray cat in the compound. It seems to have its own schedule: morning, it eats at the first-floor old lady's door; noon, it sleeps on the third-floor balcony; afternoon, it wanders the garden. Although nobody owns it, everyone seems to take care of it. It's freer than all of us.

questions.q_en: Where is the cat at noon?
Who is the cat's owner?
How does 'I' compare the cat to people?

options.text_en: cat is freer than all of us
cat is the same as people

title: The Closed Bookshop
text_zh: 路口的那家书店关了。以前每次走过那里，都能看到里面亮着灯。老板总是坐在最里面看书。现在门上贴了一张纸，上面写着「谢谢大家这些年」。我站在门口看了一会儿，觉得这条街少了什么。
text_pinyin: Lùkǒu de nà jiā shūdiàn guān le. Yǐqián měi cì zǒu guò nàli, dōu néng kàn dào lǐmiàn liàngzhe dēng. Lǎobǎn zǒngshì zuò zài zuì lǐmiàn kàn shū. Xiànzài mén shàng tiē le yì zhāng zhǐ, shàngmiàn xiězhe「xièxie dàjiā zhèxiē nián」. Wǒ zhàn zài ménkǒu kàn le yíhuìr, juéde zhè tiáo jiē shǎo le shénme.
text_en: The bookshop at the intersection has closed. Before, every time I walked past, the lights were on inside. The owner always sat in the back reading. Now there's a note on the door: 'Thank you everyone for these years.' I stood at the entrance for a while, feeling like something was missing from this street.

questions.q_zh: 书店的老板以前总是在做什么？
questions.q_en: What did the bookshop owner always do?

options.pinyin: zuò zài zuì lǐmiàn kàn shū

questions.q_en: What did the note on the door say?

options.text_en: thank you everyone for these years

options.pinyin: zài xiū, hěn kuài jiù hǎo

questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel standing at the entrance?

options.pinyin: juéde zhè tiáo jiē shǎo le shénme
options.text_en: felt something was missing from the street

title: The Alley Between Buildings
text_zh: 两栋楼之间有一条很窄的小巷。大多数人都不走那里。但是如果你走进去，会发现一个老人在那里种花。花长在各种各样的旧东西里——旧鞋、旧杯子、旧碗。他说：「什么东西都可以种花，别扔了。」
text_pinyin: Liǎng dòng lóu zhījiān yǒu yì tiáo hěn zhǎi de xiǎo xiàng. Dàduōshù rén dōu bù zǒu nàli. Dànshì rúguǒ nǐ zǒu jìnqù, huì fāxiàn yí gè lǎorén zài nàli zhòng huā. Huā zhǎng zài gè zhǒng gè yàng de jiù dōngxi lǐ——jiù xié, jiù bēizi, jiù wǎn. Tā shuō:「Shénme dōngxi dōu kěyǐ zhòng huā, bié rēng le.」
text_en: Between two buildings there's a very narrow alley. Most people don't go there. But if you walk in, you'll find an old man growing flowers. The flowers grow in all sorts of old things — old shoes, old cups, old bowls. He says: 'You can grow flowers in anything. Don't throw things away.'

questions.q_en: Where is the alley?
What do the flowers grow in?
What did the old man say?

options.text: 什么东西都可以种花，别扔了
options.pinyin: shénme dōngxi dōu kěyǐ zhòng huā, bié rēng le
options.text_en: you can grow flowers in anything, don't throw things away

options.pinyin: zhèxiē huā shì wǒ mǎi de

title: The Crosswalk
text_zh: 红灯的时候，大家都站在路口等。有的人看手机，有的人看对面。绿灯一亮，大家一起走过去。从这边到那边，只要二十秒。在这二十秒里，很多不认识的人一起走在同一条路上。然后到了对面，大家又各走各的路了。
text_pinyin: Hóng dēng de shíhou, dàjiā dōu zhàn zài lùkǒu děng. Yǒude rén kàn shǒujī, yǒude rén kàn duìmiàn. Lǜ dēng yí liàng, dàjiā yìqǐ zǒu guòqù. Cóng zhè biān dào nà biān, zhǐ yào èrshí miǎo. Zài zhè èrshí miǎo lǐ, hěn duō bú rènshi de rén yìqǐ zǒu zài tóng yì tiáo lù shàng. Ránhòu dào le duìmiàn, dàjiā yòu gè zǒu gè de lù le.
text_en: When the light is red, everyone stands at the intersection waiting. Some look at phones, some watch across. When green lights up, everyone walks across together. From this side to that — only twenty seconds. In those twenty seconds, many strangers walk the same path together. Then they reach the other side and go their separate ways.

questions.q_zh: 从这边走到那边要多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long to cross?

questions.q_zh: 过马路的人互相认识吗？
questions.q_en: Do the people crossing know each other?

questions.q_zh: 过了马路以后，大家怎么做？
questions.q_en: What does everyone do after crossing?

title: The Corner Store Owner
text_zh: 街角有一个小店，什么都卖。老板是一个中年女人，一天到晚都在店里。她认识住在这里的每一个人。你买东西的时候，她会说：「你妈妈昨天也来买了这个。」或者「你们家的小孩上学了吧？」她知道的比谁都多。
text_pinyin: Jiē jiǎo yǒu yí gè xiǎo diàn, shénme dōu mài. Lǎobǎn shì yí gè zhōngnián nǚrén, yì tiān dào wǎn dōu zài diàn lǐ. Tā rènshi zhù zài zhèlǐ de měi yí gè rén. Nǐ mǎi dōngxi de shíhou, tā huì shuō:「Nǐ māma zuótiān yě lái mǎi le zhège.」Huòzhě「nǐmen jiā de xiǎohái shàng xué le ba?」Tā zhīdào de bǐ shéi dōu duō.
text_en: On the corner there's a small shop that sells everything. The owner is a middle-aged woman who's in the shop from morning to night. She knows every person who lives here. When you buy something, she'll say: 'Your mom bought this yesterday too.' Or: 'Your kid has started school, right?' She knows more than anyone.

questions.q_en: What does this shop sell?
Who does the owner know?

options.pinyin: zhù zài zhèlǐ de měi yí gè rén

questions.q_zh: 老板知道什么样的事情？
questions.q_en: What kind of things does the owner know?

options.pinyin: měi gè rén jiā lǐ de shì

title: The Sound of the City Waking Up
text_zh: 如果你五点起来，会听到城市醒来的声音。先是扫地的人，然后是早饭店开门的声音。接着是公共汽车开过的声音。最后是人说话的声音。每天都是这个顺序，从来不变。我觉得一个城市的早上比晚上更真实。
text_pinyin: Rúguǒ nǐ wǔ diǎn qǐlái, huì tīng dào chéngshì xǐng lái de shēngyīn. Xiān shì sǎo dì de rén, ránhòu shì zǎofàn diàn kāi mén de shēngyīn. Jiēzhe shì gōnggòng qìchē kāi guò de shēngyīn. Zuìhòu shì rén shuō huà de shēngyīn. Měi tiān dōu shì zhège shùnxù, cónglái bú biàn. Wǒ juéde yí gè chéngshì de zǎoshang bǐ wǎnshang gèng zhēnshí.
text_en: If you wake up at five, you hear the city waking up. First the sweepers, then the breakfast shops opening. Then buses passing. Finally, people talking. Every day the same order, never changing. I think a city's morning is more real than its night.

questions.q_zh: 城市早上最先听到什么声音？
questions.q_en: What's the first sound in the morning?

questions.q_zh: 这些声音的顺序会变吗？
questions.q_en: Does the order of sounds change?

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得什么时候的城市更真实？
questions.q_en: When does 'I' think the city is more real?

title: The Bus Stop Conversations
text_zh: 等公共汽车的时候，有时候旁边的人会跟你说话。今天一个老太太问我几点了。我告诉她以后，她又问我去哪里。然后她说她女儿也住在那个方向。等车来的时候，我已经知道了她女儿的工作、她家的猫、还有她最喜欢的饭馆。
text_pinyin: Děng gōnggòng qìchē de shíhou, yǒu shíhou pángbiān de rén huì gēn nǐ shuō huà. Jīntiān yí gè lǎo tàitai wèn wǒ jǐ diǎn le. Wǒ gàosu tā yǐhòu, tā yòu wèn wǒ qù nǎli. Ránhòu tā shuō tā nǚ'ér yě zhù zài nàge fāngxiàng. Děng chē lái de shíhou, wǒ yǐjīng zhīdào le tā nǚ'ér de gōngzuò, tā jiā de māo, háiyǒu tā zuì xǐhuan de fànguǎn.
text_en: While waiting for the bus, sometimes the person next to you talks to you. Today an old lady asked me what time it was. After I told her, she asked where I was going. Then she said her daughter also lives in that direction. By the time the bus came, I already knew her daughter's job, her cat, and her favorite restaurant.

questions.q_en: What did the old lady ask first?

questions.q_zh: 老太太的女儿住在哪里？
questions.q_en: Where does the old lady's daughter live?

options.pinyin: gēn wǒ tóng yí gè fāngxiàng

questions.q_zh: 车来的时候，「我」知道了什么？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' know by the time the bus came?

options.pinyin: zhǐ zhīdào tā zhù zài nǎli

options.text: 她女儿的工作、她家的猫和她最喜欢的饭馆
options.pinyin: tā nǚ'ér de gōngzuò, tā jiā de māo hé tā zuì xǐhuan de fànguǎn
options.text_en: daughter's job, her cat, favorite restaurant

title: The Office Thermostat War
text_zh: 我们办公室每天都有一个问题：空调的温度。坐在窗旁边的人觉得太热，要开低一点。坐在里面的人觉得太冷，要开高一点。每天有人调高，就有人调低。最后大家决定轮流管空调——一天一个人。但是问题还是一样。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen bàngōngshì měi tiān dōu yǒu yí gè wèntí: kōngtiáo de wēndù. Zuò zài chuāng pángbiān de rén juéde tài rè, yào kāi dī yìdiǎn. Zuò zài lǐmiàn de rén juéde tài lěng, yào kāi gāo yìdiǎn. Měi tiān yǒu rén tiáo gāo, jiù yǒu rén tiáo dī. Zuìhòu dàjiā juédìng lúnliú guǎn kōngtiáo——yì tiān yí gè rén. Dànshì wèntí háishi yíyàng.
text_en: Our office has a daily problem: the air conditioner temperature. People by the window think it's too hot, wanting it lower. People inside think it's too cold, wanting it higher. Every day someone turns it up, someone turns it down. Finally everyone decided to take turns controlling it — one person per day. But the problem is still the same.

questions.q_zh: 办公室每天有什么问题？
questions.q_en: What daily problem does the office have?

questions.q_zh: 坐在窗旁边的人觉得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How do the people by the window feel?

questions.q_zh: 大家的解决办法有用吗？
questions.q_en: Did their solution work?

options.text_en: no, problem is still the same

title: The School Notice Board
text_zh: 学校门口有一个公告栏。上面有很多纸条。有找猫的，有卖自行车的，有找人一起学英语的。我最喜欢看的是一张很旧的纸条，上面写着：「找一个人一起吃午饭。」下面只有一个电话号码。我不知道这个人找到人了没有，但是我觉得这是最诚实的一张。
text_pinyin: Xuéxiào ménkǒu yǒu yí gè gōnggào lán. Shàngmiàn yǒu hěn duō zhǐtiáo. Yǒu zhǎo māo de, yǒu mài zìxíngchē de, yǒu zhǎo rén yìqǐ xué Yīngyǔ de. Wǒ zuì xǐhuan kàn de shì yì zhāng hěn jiù de zhǐtiáo, shàngmiàn xiězhe:「Zhǎo yí gè rén yìqǐ chī wǔfàn.」Xiàmiàn zhǐ yǒu yí gè diànhuà hàomǎ. Wǒ bù zhīdào zhège rén zhǎo dào rén le méiyǒu, dànshì wǒ juéde zhè shì zuì chéngshí de yì zhāng.
text_en: There's a notice board at the school entrance. Many notes on it. Some looking for lost cats, some selling bicycles, some looking for English study partners. My favorite is an old note that reads: 'Looking for someone to eat lunch together.' Just a phone number below. I don't know if they found someone, but I think it's the most honest note.

questions.q_zh: 公告栏上有什么样的纸条？
questions.q_en: What kinds of notes are on the board?

options.text: 找猫的、卖自行车的、找学习同伴的
options.pinyin: zhǎo māo de, mài zìxíngchē de, zhǎo xuéxí tóngbàn de
options.text_en: lost cats, selling bikes, study partners

questions.q_en: Which note is 'I's favorite?

options.pinyin: zhǎo rén yìqǐ chī wǔfàn de

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么觉得那张纸条特别？
questions.q_en: Why does 'I' think that note is special?

title: The Company's Best Day
text_zh: 我们公司每个星期五下午三点以后不开会。这是公司最老的规矩。有人说这个规矩是因为以前的老板觉得星期五下午大家都想回家。因为这样，星期五下午变成了大家最喜欢的时间。没有人打电话，没有人发消息，大家安安静静地做自己的事情。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen gōngsī měi gè xīngqī wǔ xiàwǔ sān diǎn yǐhòu bù kāi huì. Zhè shì gōngsī zuì lǎo de guījǔ. Yǒu rén shuō zhège guījǔ shì yīnwèi yǐqián de lǎobǎn juéde xīngqī wǔ xiàwǔ dàjiā dōu xiǎng huí jiā. Yīnwèi zhèyàng, xīngqī wǔ xiàwǔ biànchéng le dàjiā zuì xǐhuan de shíjiān. Méiyǒu rén dǎ diànhuà, méiyǒu rén fā xiāoxi, dàjiā ān'ānjìngjìng de zuò zìjǐ de shìqing.
text_en: Our company doesn't hold meetings after 3 PM on Fridays. It's the oldest rule in the company. Some say it started because the former boss felt everyone wanted to go home on Friday afternoons. Because of this, Friday afternoon became everyone's favorite time. Nobody calls, nobody messages. Everyone quietly does their own thing.

questions.q_zh: 星期五下午三点以后不做什么？
questions.q_en: What doesn't happen after 3 PM on Fridays?
How did this rule originate?

options.text: 以前的老板觉得大家想回家
options.pinyin: yǐqián de lǎobǎn juéde dàjiā xiǎng huí jiā
options.text_en: former boss felt everyone wanted to go home

questions.q_zh: 星期五下午大家做什么？
questions.q_en: What does everyone do on Friday afternoon?

options.text: 安安静静做自己的事情

title: The Park's Morning Radio
text_zh: 每天早上六点半，公园的广播就开了。放的是做早操的音乐。虽然现在年轻人都不做早操了，但是老人们还是每天来。音乐一响，他们就开始动。这个广播已经放了十几年了，音乐从来没有换过。也许对他们来说，换了音乐就不一样了。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang liù diǎn bàn, gōngyuán de guǎngbō jiù kāi le. Fàng de shì zuò zǎocāo de yīnyuè. Suīrán xiànzài niánqīng rén dōu bú zuò zǎocāo le, dànshì lǎorénmen háishi měi tiān lái. Yīnyuè yí xiǎng, tāmen jiù kāishǐ dòng. Zhège guǎngbō yǐjīng fàng le shí jǐ nián le, yīnyuè cónglái méiyǒu huàn guò. Yěxǔ duì tāmen lái shuō, huàn le yīnyuè jiù bù yíyàng le.
text_en: Every morning at six-thirty, the park broadcast starts. It plays morning exercise music. Although young people don't do morning exercises anymore, the elderly still come every day. When the music starts, they begin to move. This broadcast has played for over ten years. The music has never changed. Perhaps for them, different music wouldn't be the same.

questions.q_en: When does the broadcast start?
Who still does morning exercises?
Has the music been changed?

title: The GPS Argument
text_zh: 爸爸开车的时候用手机导航。导航说往左，爸爸觉得应该往右。妈妈说：「听导航的。」爸爸说：「我在这里住了二十年了，我比手机知道得多。」最后我们走错了路。爸爸安静了五分钟，然后又开了导航。
text_pinyin: Bàba kāi chē de shíhou yòng shǒujī dǎoháng. Dǎoháng shuō wǎng zuǒ, bàba juéde yīnggāi wǎng yòu. Māma shuō:「Tīng dǎoháng de.」Bàba shuō:「Wǒ zài zhèlǐ zhù le èrshí nián le, wǒ bǐ shǒujī zhīdào de duō.」Zuìhòu wǒmen zǒu cuò le lù. Bàba ānjìng le wǔ fēnzhōng, ránhòu yòu kāi le dǎoháng.
text_en: When Dad drives, he uses phone navigation. The GPS said go left; Dad thought it should be right. Mom said: 'Listen to the GPS.' Dad said: 'I've lived here twenty years. I know more than a phone.' We ended up going the wrong way. Dad was quiet for five minutes, then turned the GPS back on.

questions.q_en: Which way did the GPS say to go?

questions.q_zh: 爸爸为什么觉得自己对？
questions.q_en: Why did Dad think he was right?

options.text_en: because he looked at a map

options.text: 因为他在这里住了二十年
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā zài zhèlǐ zhù le èrshí nián

questions.q_zh: 走错路以后爸爸怎么做了？
questions.q_en: What did Dad do after going the wrong way?

options.text: 安静了五分钟，然后又开了导航
options.pinyin: ānjìng le wǔ fēnzhōng, ránhòu yòu kāi le dǎoháng
options.text_en: was quiet 5 min, turned GPS back on

title: The Photo That Won't Work
text_zh: 我们一家人去公园拍照。妈妈让大家站好，说「一二三」。但是每次都有人没准备好。第一次弟弟在看别的地方。第二次爸爸的眼睛闭着。第三次我在笑。拍了十多次以后，妈妈说：「算了，这些不完美的照片可能比完美的更好看。」
text_pinyin: Wǒmen yì jiā rén qù gōngyuán pāi zhào. Māma ràng dàjiā zhàn hǎo, shuō「yī èr sān」. Dànshì měi cì dōu yǒu rén méi zhǔnbèi hǎo. Dì yī cì dìdi zài kàn biéde dìfang. Dì èr cì bàba de yǎnjing bìzhe. Dì sān cì wǒ zài xiào. Pāi le shí duō cì yǐhòu, māma shuō:「Suàn le, zhèxiē bù wánměi de zhàopiàn kěnéng bǐ wánměi de gèng hǎokàn.」
text_en: Our whole family went to the park to take photos. Mom told everyone to stand ready and said 'one two three.' But every time someone wasn't ready. First time, my brother was looking elsewhere. Second time, Dad's eyes were closed. Third time, I was laughing. After more than ten tries, Mom said: 'Forget it. These imperfect photos are probably better than perfect ones.'

questions.q_zh: 第一次拍照的时候，谁没准备好？
questions.q_en: Who wasn't ready the first time?
How many times did they take photos?
What did Mom say in the end?

options.text: 不完美的照片可能更好看
options.pinyin: bù wánměi de zhàopiàn kěnéng gèng hǎokàn

title: The Alarm That Doesn't Work
text_zh: 我设了三个闹钟：六点、六点十分、六点二十分。第一个响的时候，我关了继续睡。第二个响的时候，我又关了。第三个响的时候，我把手机放到了很远的地方。但是我起来关了以后，又回去睡了。最后叫醒我的是妈妈。她比所有的闹钟都管用。
text_pinyin: Wǒ shè le sān gè nàozhōng: liù diǎn, liù diǎn shí fēn, liù diǎn èrshí fēn. Dì yī gè xiǎng de shíhou, wǒ guān le jìxù shuì. Dì èr gè xiǎng de shíhou, wǒ yòu guān le. Dì sān gè xiǎng de shíhou, wǒ bǎ shǒujī fàng dào le hěn yuǎn de dìfang. Dànshì wǒ qǐlái guān le yǐhòu, yòu huíqù shuì le. Zuìhòu jiào xǐng wǒ de shì māma. Tā bǐ suǒyǒu de nàozhōng dōu guǎnyòng.
text_en: I set three alarms: six, six-ten, six-twenty. When the first one rang, I turned it off and kept sleeping. When the second rang, I turned it off again. For the third, I put my phone far away. But after getting up to turn it off, I went back to sleep. In the end, it was Mom who woke me. She's more effective than all the alarms.

questions.q_en: How many alarms did 'I' set?

questions.q_zh: 第三个闹钟的时候「我」怎么做的？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' do for the third alarm?

options.pinyin: qǐlái guān le yòu huíqù shuì le
options.text_en: got up, turned it off, went back to sleep

questions.q_en: Who finally woke 'I' up?

title: The Cooking Competition
text_zh: 我和哥哥比赛做饭。我做了炒饭，他做了面条。我们让妈妈来尝。妈妈先吃了我的，说：「还可以。」然后吃了哥哥的，也说：「还可以。」我们问她哪个更好吃。她想了很久，说：「都不怎么好吃，但是我很高兴你们会做饭了。」
text_pinyin: Wǒ hé gēge bǐsài zuò fàn. Wǒ zuò le chǎofàn, tā zuò le miàntiáo. Wǒmen ràng māma lái cháng. Māma xiān chī le wǒ de, shuō:「Hái kěyǐ.」Ránhòu chī le gēge de, yě shuō:「Hái kěyǐ.」Wǒmen wèn tā nǎge gèng hǎochī. Tā xiǎng le hěn jiǔ, shuō:「Dōu bù zěnme hǎochī, dànshì wǒ hěn gāoxìng nǐmen huì zuò fàn le.」
text_en: My brother and I had a cooking competition. I made fried rice, he made noodles. We had Mom taste them. She tried mine first and said: 'It's okay.' Then she tried my brother's and said: 'It's okay.' We asked which was better. She thought for a long time: 'Neither is very good, but I'm happy you can cook now.'

questions.q_en: What did 'I' cook?

questions.q_zh: 妈妈觉得谁做的更好吃？
questions.q_en: Whose cooking did Mom think was better?

questions.q_zh: 妈妈高兴的原因是什么？
questions.q_en: Why was Mom happy?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tāmen huì zuò fàn le
yīnwèi bú yòng zìjǐ zuò fàn
options.text_en: because she doesn't have to cook

title: The Wrong Floor
text_zh: 昨天晚上很累，我走到五楼，用钥匙开门——开不了。试了三次都不行。我正要打电话给房东，突然看到门上的号码：502。我住的是602。我上了一层楼，门一下子就开了。希望五楼的邻居没有听到我在他们门口试了三次钥匙。
text_pinyin: Zuótiān wǎnshang hěn lèi, wǒ zǒu dào wǔ lóu, yòng yàoshi kāi mén——kāi bù liǎo. Shì le sān cì dōu bù xíng. Wǒ zhèng yào dǎ diànhuà gěi fángdōng, tūrán kàn dào mén shàng de hàomǎ: wǔ líng èr. Wǒ zhù de shì liù líng èr. Wǒ shàng le yì céng lóu, mén yíxiàzi jiù kāi le. Xīwàng wǔ lóu de línjū méiyǒu tīng dào wǒ zài tāmen ménkǒu shì le sān cì yàoshi.
text_en: Last night I was tired. I walked to the fifth floor and tried to open the door with my key — it wouldn't open. Tried three times, no luck. Just as I was about to call the landlord, I noticed the number on the door: 502. I live in 602. I went up one floor and the door opened right away. I hope the fifth-floor neighbor didn't hear me trying their lock three times.

questions.q_en: What floor did 'I' go to?
Where does 'I' live?
Why did 'I' go to the wrong floor?

title: The Breakfast That Tells Time
text_zh: 我不用看表就知道几点了。六点，卖豆浆的人来了。六点半，卖包子的开门了。七点，买早饭的人排队了。七点半，卖早饭的人开始忙起来了。我妈妈说：「你不是在看时间，你是在看肚子。」她说得对。
text_pinyin: Wǒ bú yòng kàn biǎo jiù zhīdào jǐ diǎn le. Liù diǎn, mài dòujiāng de rén lái le. Liù diǎn bàn, mài bāozi de kāi mén le. Qī diǎn, mǎi zǎofàn de rén páiduì le. Qī diǎn bàn, mài zǎofàn de rén kāishǐ máng qǐlái le. Wǒ māma shuō:「Nǐ búshì zài kàn shíjiān, nǐ shì zài kàn dùzi.」Tā shuō de duì.
text_en: I can tell the time without looking at a watch. At six, the soy milk seller arrives. At six-thirty, the bun shop opens. At seven, people line up for breakfast. At seven-thirty, the sellers get busy. My mom says: 'You're not watching the time, you're watching your stomach.' She's right.

questions.q_en: What happens at six-thirty?

options.pinyin: mài dòujiāng de rén lái le
mài bāozi de kāi mén le
mài zǎofàn de rén máng qǐlái le

questions.q_en: What does Mom think 'I' is watching?
Does 'I' agree with Mom?

title: The Secret Menu
text_zh: 这个饭馆的菜单上有二十个菜。但是老客人都知道，还有三个菜没有写在上面。你要跟老板说「来个老样子」，他就知道做什么了。我去了很多次以后，终于有一天老板问我：「要不要老样子？」那一天，我觉得自己变成了这里的人。
text_pinyin: Zhège fànguǎn de càidān shàng yǒu èrshí gè cài. Dànshì lǎo kèrén dōu zhīdào, hái yǒu sān gè cài méiyǒu xiě zài shàngmiàn. Nǐ yào gēn lǎobǎn shuō「lái gè lǎo yàngzi」, tā jiù zhīdào zuò shénme le. Wǒ qù le hěn duō cì yǐhòu, zhōngyú yǒu yì tiān lǎobǎn wèn wǒ:「Yào bu yào lǎo yàngzi?」Nà yì tiān, wǒ juéde zìjǐ biànchéng le zhèlǐ de rén.
text_en: This restaurant's menu has twenty dishes. But regulars know there are three more not listed. You tell the owner 'the usual' and he knows what to make. After going many times, one day the owner finally asked me: 'Want the usual?' That day, I felt like I'd become a local.

questions.q_en: How many dishes are on the menu?
How do you order the hidden dishes?

options.pinyin: gēn lǎobǎn shuō「lái gè lǎo yàngzi」

questions.q_zh: 老板问「要不要老样子」的时候，「我」有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel when the owner asked about 'the usual'?

options.text: 觉得自己变成了这里的人
options.pinyin: juéde zìjǐ biànchéng le zhèlǐ de rén

title: Tea vs Coffee
text_zh: 爸爸喝茶，妈妈喝咖啡。他们从来不喝对方的。爸爸说茶让人安静，妈妈说咖啡让人有力气。有一天我问他们要不要试试对方的。爸爸喝了一口咖啡，说：「太苦了。」妈妈喝了一口茶，说：「没有味道。」然后他们笑了，继续喝自己的。
text_pinyin: Bàba hē chá, māma hē kāfēi. Tāmen cónglái bù hē duìfāng de. Bàba shuō chá ràng rén ānjìng, māma shuō kāfēi ràng rén yǒu lìqi. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ wèn tāmen yào bu yào shìshi duìfāng de. Bàba hē le yì kǒu kāfēi, shuō:「Tài kǔ le.」Māma hē le yì kǒu chá, shuō:「Méiyǒu wèidào.」Ránhòu tāmen xiào le, jìxù hē zìjǐ de.
text_en: Dad drinks tea, Mom drinks coffee. They never drink each other's. Dad says tea makes people calm, Mom says coffee gives energy. One day I asked if they'd try each other's. Dad took a sip of coffee: 'Too bitter.' Mom took a sip of tea: 'No flavor.' Then they laughed and went back to their own.

questions.q_en: What does Dad think about tea?
What did Dad think of coffee?
What did they do in the end?

title: Why We Say 'Hello' on the Phone
text_zh: 你有没有想过，为什么中国人打电话的时候都说「喂」？「喂」这个字本来是用来叫人的。因为以前的电话声音不太清楚，所以人们要先说一个字让对方知道电话通了。后来大家都说「喂」了，就变成了打电话的习惯。现在虽然电话的声音已经很清楚了，但是大家还是会说。
text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méi yǒu xiǎng guò, wèishénme Zhōngguó rén dǎ diànhuà de shíhou dōu shuō「wèi」? 「Wèi」zhège zì běnlái shì yòng lái jiào rén de. Yīnwèi yǐqián de diànhuà shēngyīn bú tài qīngchu, suǒyǐ rénmen yào xiān shuō yí gè zì ràng duìfāng zhīdào diànhuà tōng le. Hòulái dàjiā dōu shuō「wèi」le, jiù biànchéng le dǎ diànhuà de xíguàn. Xiànzài suīrán diànhuà de shēngyīn yǐjīng hěn qīngchu le, dànshì dàjiā háishi huì shuō.
text_en: Have you ever wondered why Chinese people say 'wei' when answering the phone? The character 'wei' originally means to call someone's attention. Because old phones had unclear sound, people needed to say a word first to let the other person know the call connected. Later everyone said 'wei' and it became a phone habit. Now the sound is clear, but people still say it.

questions.q_zh: 「喂」这个字本来是做什么用的？
questions.q_en: What was 'wei' originally used for?
Why did people used to say 'wei' first?

questions.q_zh: 现在电话声音怎么样了？
questions.q_en: How is phone sound quality now?

title: Why Left and Right Are Different
text_zh: 大多数人用右手写字，但是有的人用左手。用左手的人比用右手的少很多。为什么会这样？没有人完全知道。但是有一个有意思的事情：用左手的人做有的事情的时候，比用右手的人快。所以用左手并不是不好——只是跟别人不一样。
text_pinyin: Dàduōshù rén yòng yòu shǒu xiě zì, dànshì yǒude rén yòng zuǒ shǒu. Yòng zuǒ shǒu de rén bǐ yòng yòu shǒu de shǎo hěn duō. Wèishénme huì zhèyàng? Méiyǒu rén wánquán zhīdào. Dànshì yǒu yí gè yǒu yìsi de shìqing: yòng zuǒ shǒu de rén zuò yǒude shìqing de shíhou, bǐ yòng yòu shǒu de rén kuài. Suǒyǐ yòng zuǒ shǒu bìng búshì bù hǎo——zhǐshì gēn biéren bù yíyàng.
text_en: Most people write with their right hand, but some use the left. Left-handed people are far fewer. Why? Nobody fully knows. But here's something interesting: left-handed people are faster at certain tasks. So being left-handed isn't bad — just different from others.

questions.q_zh: 用左手的人多还是用右手的人多？
questions.q_en: Are there more left-handed or right-handed people?
Why do some people use their left hand?

questions.q_zh: 用左手的人有什么特别的？
questions.q_en: What's special about left-handed people?

options.text: 做有的事情比右手的人快
options.pinyin: zuò yǒude shìqing bǐ yòu shǒu de rén kuài
